name: config
context: Install, configure, and customize Tailwind CSS — theme, content detection, PostCSS, Vite plugin, and v3/v4 setup
info: This skill covers Tailwind project setup end-to-end: installation via npm (PostCSS plugin or Vite plugin), CSS entry point configuration, theme customization (colors, spacing, fonts, breakpoints), content source detection, and build pipeline integration. In v4, configuration moved from JavaScript (tailwind.config.js) to CSS (@theme directive) — this skill covers both approaches since agents encounter both v3 and v4 projects. Agents use this skill when bootstrapping new projects, adding Tailwind to existing codebases, customizing design tokens, or debugging build issues where classes aren't generating. See [[tailwind/classes]] for applying utilities, [[tailwind/plugins]] for extending with plugins, and [[tailwind/v4]] for the full v4 migration story.
triggers: set up tailwind in my project

sections.title: v4 installation
sections.body: Install with `npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite` (Vite) or `npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss` (PostCSS). Add the plugin to your build config. Create a CSS file with `@import "tailwindcss";`. No config file needed — customization happens in CSS via `@theme`. Content detection is automatic.

sections.title: Theme customization with @theme
sections.body: The `@theme` directive defines design tokens as CSS variables that generate utility classes. Add `--color-brand: oklch(0.6 0.2 260);` to get `bg-brand`, `text-brand`, etc. Each namespace (`--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--breakpoint-*`, `--spacing`, `--radius-*`, `--shadow-*`, `--ease-*`, `--animate-*`) maps to specific utility prefixes. Use `--namespace-*: initial;` to remove all defaults in that namespace before adding custom values.

sections.title: Content source detection
sections.body: v4 auto-scans source files respecting `.gitignore`. Use `@source "../path"` to add directories not auto-detected (e.g. external UI libraries in node_modules). v3 requires a `content` array in `tailwind.config.js` listing glob patterns for all files containing Tailwind classes. Dynamic class construction (`bg-${var}`) breaks scanning — always use complete class strings.

sections.title: v3 JavaScript configuration
sections.body: v3 uses `tailwind.config.js` with `content`, `theme`, and `plugins` fields. Run `npx tailwindcss init -p` to scaffold. Customize via `theme.extend` to add values without removing defaults, or `theme` directly to replace. CSS entry uses `@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;`. See [[tailwind/v4]] for migration to CSS-first config.

sections.title: Debugging missing classes
sections.body: Classes not appearing in output: (1) check content paths match source files, (2) verify CSS import in app entry, (3) never construct class names dynamically — use complete strings, (4) use `@source` for gitignored directories in v4, (5) confirm build tool (PostCSS or Vite) is configured and running. Browser devtools: search for the expected class in the generated stylesheet to confirm it exists.
